yojana.meaning: a yojana, the common ancient measure of distance — as far as could be travelled by one yoke (of oxen), about seven miles
breakdown: Noun suffix: the distance covered by one yoking — a yojana.

yojanagaṇanāni.meaning: (distances) counted in yojanas; many yojanas in measure
breakdown: A yojana (measure of about seven miles).
Root: to join, bind. yoni: womb; place of origin, form of rebirth.

yottaṁ.meaning: the tie of the yoke; a strap, cord
breakdown: Noun suffix; yotta: the fastening of the yoke.

yuddhakathaṁ.meaning: talk about war; conversation about battles (a kind of unsuitable talk)
breakdown: Fight, war (past participle of yujjhati, to fight).
Root: to yoke, join. yuga: the yoke that joins draught animals.

yugāni.gram: neuter noun, nominative/accusative plural of yuga
breakdown: Root: to yoke, join. yuga: yoke; by extension a pair (as in “the four pairs of persons”).

yuttaṭṭhāne.meaning: in the connected (appropriate) place; where it is fitting
breakdown: Connected, yoked; fitting, proper (past participle of yuñjati).

yācayogo.meaning: devoted to those who ask; open-handed, accessible to beggars
breakdown: Engaged in, devoted to. Together: devoted to giving when asked — open-handed.

yānassa.gram: neuter noun, genitive/dative singular of yāna
breakdown: Root: to go. yāna: vehicle, carriage, means of going.

yānīdha.gram: sandhi fusion: relative pronoun plural + adverb (yāni + idha)

yāpanīyaṁ.meaning: supporting, sustaining; suitable for keeping going (of health: bearable)
yāpanīyaṁ.gram: future passive participle of yāpeti, neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Root: to go. Causative yāpe: to keep going, sustain.
Future passive participle suffix: that by which one can keep going.

yāpenti.meaning: they live on, they sustain themselves (by)
yāpenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, causative of yāti (√yā)
breakdown: Root: to go. Causative stem yāpe: to keep going, subsist.

yāpeti.meaning: makes go; keeps going, lives by, subsists
yāpeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of yāti (√yā)
breakdown: Causative stem + 3rd person singular ending: to cause to go — to sustain oneself.

yāva.meaning: as far as, up to, until

breakdown: As far as, as long as.

yāvatassa.meaning: as long as; for as long as (for him)
breakdown: As long as, as far as.

yāvatassa as long.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply yāvatassa, “as long as”.

yāvatā.meaning: as far as; to whatever extent

ñāpetabbo.meaning: to be made known, to be announced
ñāpetabbo.gram: future passive participle of ñāpeti (causative of jānāti, √ñā), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root: to know. Causative ñāpe: to make known.
Future passive participle suffix: “to be done”.

ñātakānaṁ.gram: masculine noun, genitive/dative plural of ñātaka
breakdown: Noun suffix; ñātaka: one who is known — a relative.
Blood-relation (sa “with” + lohita “blood”: of the same blood).

ñāyapaṭipanno.meaning: practising the right way; entered upon the proper method (an epithet of the Saṅgha)
breakdown: The right way, the proper method; wisdom.
Entered upon, practising (past participle of paṭipajjati).

ñāyaṁ.meaning: the right way, the true method (accusative); in the lexicon's parsing of the verse context: “not this” (na + ayaṁ)
ñāyaṁ.gram: masculine noun ñāya, accusative singular; or sandhi of na + ayaṁ as the lexicon parses
ñāyaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon line parses this occurrence as na + ayaṁ (“not + this”), which would normally yield nāyaṁ; as a lexeme ñāyaṁ is the accusative of ñāya, “the right way”.

ñāyaṁ dhammanti.meaning: “the right-way dhamma” — i.e. the Dhamma of the noble path (ariyamaggadhamma), with quotation marker
ñāyaṁ dhammanti.gram: phrase: noun + noun + quotation particle ti
breakdown: The right way, proper method; here referring to the noble path (ariyamagga).
Teaching, dhamma (accusative dhammaṁ; the -n- before ti is sandhi).

ñāṇakaraṇī.meaning: producing knowledge; making for true knowledge

breakdown: Root: to bear, carry. ābharaṇa: what is worn — an ornament.

ābhicetasikānaṁ.meaning: of (states) belonging to the higher mind; of clear consciousness
breakdown: Strengthened form of abhi-: higher, superior.
Adjectival suffix: belonging to. Of the higher, clear mind — said of the jhānas.
Root: to oppress, afflict. ābādha: affliction — illness.

ācariyamuṭṭhi.meaning: the teacher's fist — keeping something of the teaching back from the disciple
ācariyamuṭṭhi.summary: An idiom: a closed-fisted teacher withholds part of the teaching; the Buddha declared he had no such “teacher's fist”.
breakdown: Fist. The closed fist of a teacher: esoteric withholding of the teaching.

ācikkhanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of ācikkhati (ā + √cikkh)

breakdown: Feminine action-noun suffix: the act of pointing out.

ācikkheyya.meaning: would tell, would relate, should describe
ācikkheyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of ācikkhati

ācikkhissāmyanāgataṁ.meaning: I will tell the future; I shall announce what is to come
ācikkhissāmyanāgataṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: 1st person singular future + noun (ācikkhissāmi + anāgataṁ)
breakdown: I will tell, show, announce (future of ācikkhati; the -y- is a sandhi glide).
The future, what has not yet come (an + āgata, “not come”).

āciṇṇaṁ.gram: past participle of ācarati (ā + √car), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Past participle suffix (car + na > ciṇṇa): practised, carried out.

ācārapaññātti.meaning: regulation of right conduct; precept or ordinance concerning practice

ācārasīlaguṇavattapaṭipattiyā.meaning: by conduct, sīla, quality, observance and practice

breakdown: Feminine action-noun suffix: the act of lamenting.

ādevitattaṁ.meaning: the state of lamentation; the condition of crying and bewailing

breakdown: Root: to lament. ādeva: wailing, lamentation.

ādhānaggāhī.meaning: sticking obstinately to what one has taken up; dogmatically tenacious
breakdown: Depositing, placing down; what is laid hold of.
Taking up, seizing (the gg is junction doubling). Together: one who grips what he has taken up — obstinate.

ādikalyāṇaṁ.meaning: beneficial in the beginning; lovely at the start (said of the Dhamma)

ādimajjhapariyosānakalyāṇattā.meaning: because of being beneficial in the beginning, the middle and the end
ādimajjhapariyosānakalyāṇattā.gram: compound abstract noun, ablative singular (“because of...”)
breakdown: Abstract suffix in the ablative: because of the state of being so.

ādinā.meaning: with (this) as the beginning; and so forth (usually with nayena: “in this and similar ways”)

ādinā nayena.meaning: thus and in similar ways; by this method and the like
breakdown: With (this) as the beginning; and so forth.
By the method, in the manner (instrumental of naya).

ādittaṁ.gram: past participle of ādippati (ā + √dīp), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Past participle suffix (dīp + ta > ditta): set ablaze.

ādivassasate.meaning: in the first hundred years; in the first century

ādiyati.meaning: takes up, grasps; takes upon oneself (e.g. a precept)
breakdown: Root: to take. ādiyati: to take up, undertake.
